Thursday, May 14, 2026 Daily Digest

SAP Launches "Autonomous Enterprise," GrowthLoop: 77% of Winning Tests Fail at Scale, POSSIBLE 2026 Declares AI "Tectonic"

Today's top stories in AI + enterprise tech

1

SAP Sapphire 2026: The "Autonomous Enterprise" Arrives

At SAP Sapphire in Orlando, CEO Christian Klein unveiled the Autonomous Enterprise — SAP's biggest AI bet yet. The new SAP Business AI Platform unifies BTP, Business Data Cloud, and Business AI into one governed environment. More than 50 domain-specific Joule Assistants will automate end-to-end processes across finance, supply chain, HR, and CX. The "Autonomous Close Assistant" alone promises to compress financial close from weeks to days. SAP also committed €100M to help partners deploy it.

Read on SAP News →
2

GrowthLoop: 77% of "Winning" Marketing Experiments Fail at Scale

GrowthLoop's 2026 AI and Marketing Performance Index surveyed 300+ marketers and found a stark disconnect: despite 87% implementing AI, only 23% can reliably link marketing actions to business outcomes. The killer stat? 77% say tests that "win" in pilots fail when scaled. Companies with a single source of truth reported 44% revenue growth vs. 8% without. The message: AI amplifies speed, not intelligence — and most teams are still optimizing for the past.

Read on PRNewswire →
3

POSSIBLE 2026: AI Is "Tectonic," Not Incremental

LUMA Partners CEO Terry Kawaja delivered the standout keynote at POSSIBLE 2026 in Miami: AI isn't like mobile, social, or programmatic — it's a tectonic shift. His framework: AI's breadth (touches every role), depth (ingests fragmented data into actionable signals), and severity (restructures industry economics). Most orgs are stuck on operational improvements like workflow automation. The next layer — real-time creative optimization and conversational commerce — is where differentiation lives.

Read on Harmelin Media →
4

UiPath Makes Orchestration the AI Control Layer

UiPath launched "UiPath for Coding Agents" — claiming the first enterprise platform that natively integrates AI coding agents (Claude Code, OpenAI Codex) into production automation. The key insight: as AI-generated code becomes ubiquitous, orchestration, governance, and auditability become the control layer. Instead of picking one AI vendor, enterprises can manage multiple models through a unified runtime with security, compliance, and deployment baked in.

Read on MarTech Edge →
5

Insider One Named 2026 Gartner Peer Insights Customers' Choice

Insider One earned the 2026 Gartner Peer Insights Customers' Choice for Multichannel Marketing Hubs, with near-perfect scores across the board: 4.9/5.0 for product capabilities, sales experience, and support. As AI-powered customer engagement platforms mature, this recognition signals which vendors are actually delivering — not just demoing — enterprise-grade AI marketing automation.

View on Insider One →

💡 My Take

Read this one: The GrowthLoop report is a cold shower for anyone celebrating AI adoption metrics. 87% of marketers are using AI, but only 23% can prove it drives outcomes — and most "successful" tests collapse at scale. The winners aren't the fastest; they're the ones with unified data foundations. Speed without intelligence is just spinning faster.
